title: Lesson 41 – Giving and Receiving (Keigo)
description: Keigo-Verben fuer Geben und Empfangen: いただきます (bescheiden), くださいます (ehrerbietig), さしあげます (bescheiden), やります, ～ていただけませんか (hoefliche Bitte).

vocabulary.meaning: to give (honorific, someone gives to me)
to give (humble, I give to someone)
visiting a sick person, get-well gift

vocabulary.word: つまらないものですが
vocabulary.meaning: it's nothing special, but... (when giving a gift)

grammar.title: いただきます — Humble form of もらいます
grammar.structure: N₁ は N₂ に N₃ を いただきます
grammar.explanation: いただきます is the humble form of もらいます (to receive). It is used when the speaker (or someone in the speaker's in-group) receives something from a person of higher status. N₁ is the receiver (speaker/in-group), N₂ is the giver (person of higher status), and N₃ is the thing received. Because it is a humble expression, the subject is always 'I' or someone in the speaker's group — never the superior.
grammar.example_sentences:
① わたしは 社長に 本を いただきました。
  (Watashi wa shachou ni hon o itadakimashita.)
  — I received a book from the company president.
② 先生に 辞書を いただきました。
  (Sensei ni jisho o itadakimashita.)
  — I received a dictionary from my teacher.

grammar.title: くださいます — Honorific form of くれます
grammar.structure: N₁ が N₂ に N₃ を くださいます
grammar.explanation: くださいます is the honorific form of くれます (someone gives to me). It is used when a person of higher status gives something to the speaker (or the speaker's in-group). N₁ is the giver (person of higher status), N₂ is the receiver (speaker/in-group), and N₃ is the thing given. The subject performing the action is the respected person.
grammar.example_sentences:
③ 部長が [わたしに] お土産を くださいました。
  (Buchou ga [watashi ni] omiyage o kudasaimashita.)
  — The department manager gave me a souvenir.
④ 先生が 写真を くださいました。
  (Sensei ga shashin o kudasaimashita.)
  — The teacher gave me a photograph.

grammar.title: さしあげます / やります — Humble give / Give to inferiors
grammar.structure: N₁ は N₂ に N₃ を さしあげます / やります
grammar.explanation: さしあげます is the humble form of あげます (to give). It is used when the speaker (or someone in the speaker's in-group) gives something to a person of higher status. やります is used when giving to animals, plants, or people of clearly lower status (e.g., giving food to a pet). Note: さしあげます can sound overly humble or presumptuous in some contexts, so use with care.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑤ わたしは 先生に お花を さしあげました。
  (Watashi wa sensei ni ohana o sashiagemashita.)
  — I gave flowers to my teacher.
⑥ 犬に 餌を やりました。
  (Inu ni esa o yarimashita.)
  — I gave food to the dog.

grammar.title: ～ていただけませんか — Very polite request
grammar.structure: V て-form + いただけませんか
grammar.explanation: ～ていただけませんか is a very polite way to make a request. It literally means 'Could I receive the favor of you doing ~?' It is more polite than ～てくださいませんか or ～てください. Use this form when asking a favor of a superior, a stranger, or in formal situations.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑦ この書類を 説明していただけませんか。
  (Kono shorui o setsumei shite itadakemasen ka.)
  — Could you please explain this document?
⑧ 駅まで 送っていただけませんか。
  (Eki made okutte itadakemasen ka.)
  — Could you please take me to the station?

conversation.title: Thank you very much for this wonderful gift
conversation.title_ja: すてきなお土産をありがとうございます

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: ミラーさん、先日は すてきな お土産を いただきまして、ありがとうございました。
lines.romaji: Miraa-san, senjitsu wa suteki na omiyage o itadakimashite, arigatou gozaimashita.
lines.english: Mr. Miller, thank you very much for the wonderful souvenir the other day.

lines.speaker: Miller
lines.japanese: いいえ、つまらないものですが。お口に 合いましたか。
lines.romaji: Iie, tsumaranai mono desu ga. Okuchi ni aimashita ka.
lines.english: Not at all, it was nothing special. Did you like it?

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: ええ、家族も とても 喜んでいました。ところで、来週の 会議の 書類なんですが…
lines.romaji: Ee, kazoku mo totemo yorokonde imashita. Tokorode, raishuu no kaigi no shorui nan desu ga...
lines.english: Yes, my family was very happy too. By the way, about the documents for next week's meeting...

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: 英語の 部分を 翻訳していただけませんか。
lines.romaji: Eigo no bubun o honyaku shite itadakemasen ka.
lines.english: Could you please translate the English parts?

lines.speaker: Miller
lines.japanese: はい、もちろんです。金曜日までに お届けします。
lines.romaji: Hai, mochiron desu. Kinyoubi made ni otodoke shimasu.
lines.english: Yes, of course. I'll deliver it to you by Friday.

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: 助かります。よろしくお願いします。
lines.english: That's a great help. Thank you in advance.
